feedback.enterpriseOnly: Microsoft 365 inbound email is only available in Enterprise Edition.

description.enterprise: Configure Gmail, Microsoft 365, or IMAP providers to receive and process inbound emails as tickets
description.standard: Configure Gmail or IMAP providers to receive and process inbound emails as tickets

steps.registerApp: Register an application in Azure AD
steps.redirectUrl: Set up the redirect URL in your app registration
steps.credentials: Use the Client ID and Client Secret in the form above

enterpriseSteps.enterAddress: Enter your Gmail address and provider name

standardSteps.project: Create a project in Google Cloud Console
standardSteps.oauth: Enable Gmail API and create OAuth2 credentials
standardSteps.pubsub: Set up Pub/Sub topic for push notifications
standardSteps.consent: Configure the OAuth consent screen and add test users

header.title: Choose Your Email Provider
header.description: Select the email service you want to use for inbound email processing. You can configure multiple email providers per account.

help.enterprise: Choose the provider your organization already uses. If you use Google Workspace, pick Gmail; if you use Microsoft 365, pick Microsoft 365. You can change this later by removing and reconfiguring your email provider.
help.standard: Choose the provider your organization already uses. If you use Google Workspace, pick Gmail; otherwise choose IMAP. You can change this later by removing and reconfiguring your email provider.

warnings.inboundDefaults: Inbound ticket defaults are required and emails won't process until one is selected.

empty.title: No Email Providers Configured
empty.description: Add an email provider to start receiving and processing inbound emails as tickets.

basic.title: Basic Configuration
basic.description: Basic settings for your Gmail email provider

oauth.sectionDescription: Uses the tenant-owned Google app configured in Settings → Integrations → Providers.
oauth.notConfigured: Google integration is not configured for this tenant.
oauth.setupHelp: Configure tenant-owned Google OAuth + Pub/Sub first: Settings → Integrations → Providers.
oauth.requiredDescriptionAdd: You must complete OAuth authorization above before adding the provider to enable Gmail notifications.
oauth.requiredDescriptionUpdate: You must complete OAuth authorization above before updating the provider to enable Gmail notifications.

processing.labelFiltersHelp: Comma-separated list of Gmail labels to monitor (default: INBOX)

validation.mailboxInvalid: Valid mailbox is required (e.g. user@domain.com, user@localhost, user@test-server, or user)

basic.description: Define the IMAP mailbox connection details.

auth.title: Authentication
auth.passwordHelp: Passwords are stored securely and will not be displayed. Leave blank to keep the existing password.

validation.nameRequired: Short name and display name are required

description.label: Description
description.placeholder: Optional description of when these defaults are used

client.label: Client *
client.help: Required: used as a catch-all when no client can be matched from the email.

category.label: Category
category.empty: No categories found for the selected board.

location.label: Location
location.help: Only applied when the catch-all client is used (no match case).

enteredBy.label: Entered By
enteredBy.help: Used only when we cannot match a contact or client. System tickets will show "System" as creator.

basic.description: Define the IMAP mailbox connection details.

header.title: Microsoft 365 Integration
header.description: Simply connect your Microsoft 365 account and configure your email processing preferences to get started.

accountSetup.description: Configure your Microsoft 365 account for inbound email processing

authentication.description: Connect your Microsoft 365 account to enable email processing

messages.loadOutboundSettingsFailed: Failed to load outbound email settings
messages.ticketingFromSaveFailed: Failed to save ticketing from address
messages.ticketingFromClearFailed: Failed to clear ticketing from address
messages.domainRemovalScheduledWithClear: Domain removal scheduled and ticketing From address cleared

outbound.intro: Configure outbound email for your organization.
outbound.providerDescription: Choose how outbound emails are sent from your organization.
outbound.resendDescription: Emails are sent through Nine Minds managed infrastructure. Add and verify a custom domain below.
outbound.smtpDescription: Emails are sent through your own SMTP server.
outbound.upgradeNotice: Managed email domains are not available on your current plan. Upgrade to use Nine Minds managed outbound infrastructure with custom domains.
outbound.domainsDescription: Add a custom domain and follow the DNS instructions to verify ownership.
outbound.smtpConfigDescription: Enter your SMTP server details to send outbound email.

smtp.fromHelp: The default sender address for outbound emails.

ticketingFrom.title: Ticketing From Address
ticketingFrom.description: Choose the email address that will appear in the From header on ticket notifications.
ticketingFrom.smtpHint: Address should use a domain associated with your SMTP server ({{domain}}). Replies work best when you use a connected inbound inbox.
ticketingFrom.managedHint: Address must use your outbound domain ({{domain}}). Replies work best when you use a connected inbound inbox.
ticketingFrom.smtpRequiredMessage: Save your SMTP configuration with a from address before selecting a ticketing from address.
ticketingFrom.managedRequiredMessage: Add and verify a managed domain before selecting a ticketing from address.
ticketingFrom.connectedInboxHelp: Select a connected inbox to keep inbound replies threaded.
ticketingFrom.customSmtpHelp: Use a domain your SMTP server is authorized to send from. Using a mismatched domain may cause delivery failures or spam filtering.
ticketingFrom.customManagedHelp: Must match {{domain}}. If this isn't one of your inbound inboxes, inbound ticket processing may not work.

inbound.intro: Configure inbound email routing and provider-specific automation.

clearTicketingFrom.title: Clear Ticketing From Address
clearTicketingFrom.message: Clear the saved ticketing From address? Ticket notifications will stop using a custom From address until you save a new one.

removeDomain.title: Remove Managed Domain
removeDomain.messageWithClear: Remove {{domain}}? This will also clear the saved ticketing From address because this domain is currently active for outbound email.

validation.saveSmtpFirst: Save your SMTP configuration with a from address first
validation.addOutboundFirst: Add and verify an outbound domain before choosing a from address
validation.customAddressThreadWarning: Using a custom address may prevent inbound ticket replies from threading correctly.
validation.smtpDomainMismatchWarning: This domain does not match your SMTP from address domain ({{domain}}). Emails may fail to deliver or be flagged as spam if your SMTP server is not authorized to send from this domain.
validation.notConnectedWarning: Inbound ticket processing may not work with this address because it is not one of your connected inboxes.

domainList.empty: No managed domains yet. Add one to get started.
domainList.dnsRecordsHelp: Copy each record below into your DNS provider (GoDaddy, Cloudflare, etc.). We cannot change your DNS for you.

dnsEmpty.pending: We asked Resend to generate the DNS records for this domain. Once they show up, copy them into your DNS provider because we cannot update it automatically.
dnsEmpty.failed: We still need DNS instructions from Resend. Click Re-check DNS and, when the records load, publish them inside your DNS provider.
dnsEmpty.default: DNS instructions are not available yet. Re-check DNS and copy each record into your DNS provider as soon as it appears.

dnsRecords.defaultEmpty: We are preparing the DNS records for this domain. As soon as they appear, copy them into your DNS provider because we cannot update it for you.
dnsRecords.summaryWithCheckedAt: Matched {{matched}} of {{total}} required DNS records • Last checked {{checkedAt}}.
dnsRecords.lastCheckedNoRecords: Last checked {{checkedAt}}. We have not detected any records yet.
dnsRecords.missingHelp: We have not detected this record in DNS yet. Double-check that it exists in your DNS provider with the exact value shown above.
dnsRecords.mismatchHelp: The DNS record exists, but the value does not match what Resend expects.
dnsRecords.matchedHelp: Detected value matches what we expected.
dnsRecords.unknownHelp: We have not checked DNS yet. Re-check DNS to run verification.
